rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in core parts of the argument, including framing ERS as a validity threat and describing the established three-step correction approach in marketing. The 2008 paper then identifies specific limitations of that approach and develops an IRT model to address them (item heterogeneity, testlet dependence, and cross-country noninvariance). It also benchmarks performance against the traditional ERS operationalization associated with the cited work in simulations and empirical analyses. This role is clearly integral, not a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Baumgartner and Steenkamp (2001) as a key prior approach to response-style measurement and correction, then improves that approach with an IRT-based model that relaxes its assumptions. It substantively refines the earlier methodology by proposing and validating a more flexible measurement framework for ERS.
